frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Arre suno, humare mohalle mein wo Sharma ji ka beta hai na? Uski shaadi hui thi pichhle saal, ab sun raha hoon biwi ne 'maintenance' ka case kar diya hai. Ladka bechara, bol raha hai sab jhooth hai. Aajkal ki ladkiyan bohot chalaak ho gayi hain, shaadi karti hain paisa dekh ke, aur phir zara sa kuch hua nahi ki seedhe case kar deti hain. Sab mardo ko phansane ka naya tareeka hai ye!
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment encodes the belief that modern women exploit marriage laws for financial gain, specifically through maintenance cases, which is framed as a 'new trick' to trap men. It generalizes women as 'chalaak' (cunning) for marrying based on money, aligning with the 'golddigger' narrative and victimhood inversion.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
